checks.finding: T-001 (tasks.md:96-121) and T-002 (tasks.md:292-312) both declare Risk: medium. Proximity scan of Scope/Done When/Goal against risk-classification-policy.md's sensitive-surface list (auth, authz, payment, PII, data migration, externally-visible API contract) finds no match: T-001 edits scripts/bump-version.sh, T-002 edits .github/workflows/release.yml; design.md states 'Breaking API: no; No new contract' for both (tasks.md:150-153, 346-349). Risk Rationale names three concrete, evidence-based grounds (additive/fail-closed-only insertion point; TEST-002/003/008/009 as external mechanical negative-path verification distinct from self-certification; TEST-004/005 automatic weakened-gate detection) rather than asserting the tier, satisfying risk-classification-policy.md's Rationale requirement. No over-classification either: scope is not UI-copy/CSS/docs/test-only. medium is appropriate for both tasks.

SKIP: no task in tasks.md carries Risk: high or Risk: critical (T-001 and T-002 are both Risk: medium), so the mandatory high/critical Done-When evidence items (Red→Green TDD evidence, named independent reviewer verdict, and for critical, second approver + signed evidence bundle) do not apply to this task set.

T-001's Scope section (tasks.md:195-219) spans four distinct implementation areas: (1) core scripts/bump-version.sh edit, (2) new twin test-suite authoring across tests/bump-version-gate.tests.sh/.ps1 covering TEST-001..006 including a novel tar-copy+`git init` fixture technique, (3) CI/registration wiring across tests/run-all.sh, tests/run-all.ps1, and .github/workflows/test.yml, and (4) documentation authoring — CREATE CHANGELOG.md's #148 entry and CREATE the new docs/contributor/release-runbook.md, plus verify README.md — exceeding the '>three distinct implementation areas' oversizing indicator. T-001's Done When (tasks.md:221-265) renders as 8 top-level bullets but bundles well over 8 independently-verifiable claims once the 'Cross-cutting shares' bullet (6 sub-claims: CHANGELOG entry, runbook existence+content, absence of scripts/bump-version.ps1, REQ-004 degradation-note wording, README verification, tests/validate-repository.sh exit code) and the 'Acceptance-first evidence' bullet (pre-landing state record + red execution log + green run + independent quality-gate verdict) are unpacked. T-002 (tasks.md:387-410, 412-445) shows the same four-area pattern at smaller scale (4 TEST cases, no novel fixture technique) — a secondary, less severe instance. Concrete failure mode: a single implementer session/commit covering bash script surgery, a new git-fixture test-harness design, CI wiring in three files, and new technical documentation is unlikely to fit one focused session, raising the risk of a rushed or hard-to-review oversized diff.

T-001's acceptance criteria include three independent negative/reject paths beyond AC-001's happy path: AC-002 (loop-consistency stubbed failing), AC-003 (loop-inventory stubbed failing, independent leg), and AC-004 (no-bypass grep self-check); AC-005's ordering assertion is a supporting structural check for the same fail-closed property. T-002's acceptance criteria include AC-008 (escape-hatch negative scan) and AC-009 (negative-branch canary proving AC-008 is not vacuous) beyond AC-007's happy path. No functional task in this feature has happy-path-only acceptance coverage.

acceptance-tests.md's declared Test Type labels (integration; negative-branch; construction proof; structural ordering assertion; CI resilience + self-registration conformance; configuration conformance; negative-branch canary; document conformance; hygiene/non-existence assertion) are not literally the four canonical unit/integration/e2e/acceptance labels, but each accurately describes the actual multi-component, real-artifact-driven scope tested (e.g. TEST-001 exercises the real bump-version.sh script against a fixture-repo copy — genuinely integration-scoped; TEST-011/012's 'document conformance' targets CHANGELOG.md/release-runbook.md content, not code behavior). No test task's declared type falsely narrows or widens its actual verification scope (no 'unit' label attached to a cross-component check). No mismatch found.

SKIP: neither T-001 nor T-002 is Risk: high or Risk: critical (both Risk: medium), so this check's mandatory Rollback requirement does not apply. Noted beyond the requirement: both tasks nonetheless carry an explicit Rollback: field (tasks.md:155-158 for T-001, tasks.md:351-353 for T-002) describing a reviewed commit revert with no human-copy step required.

The files/sections edited by both T-001 and T-002 (tests/run-all.sh, tests/run-all.ps1, .github/workflows/test.yml, CHANGELOG.md's ## Unreleased #148 entry, docs/contributor/release-runbook.md) are explicitly documented as coordinated, disjoint-line edits in tasks.md Global Constraints (lines 42-60) and design.md Global Constraints (lines 393-407): each task adds only its own array entry or appends to a create-then-append single section, with ordering structurally enforced by T-002's Blockers: T-001 (tasks.md:462). This matches the legitimate multi-task scope-sharing exception (create then append, ordering enforced by Blockers). No uncoordinated overlap found.

T-002's Blockers: T-001 (tasks.md:462) is not spurious despite T-002's own 'Depends On: none materially' note (tasks.md:322-326): T-002 genuinely depends on T-001's output for two artifacts it must APPEND to rather than CREATE — CHANGELOG.md's ## Unreleased #148 entry (T-001 CREATEs, T-002 APPENDs, tasks.md:337-338) and docs/contributor/release-runbook.md (T-001 CREATEs, T-002 APPENDs, tasks.md:339-340) — plus the shared registration-array serialization documented in Global Constraints. T-001's Blockers: None is correct (no upstream task exists). No spurious or missing blocker found.

SKIP: no bugfix or debugging task in scope. Both T-001 and T-002 are net-new feature-addition tasks (adding a release-gate prerequisite), not bugfix/regression-fix/debugging/incident-remediation tasks.
